channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a range of third-party tool integrations, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This development signifies a move towards more versatile and powerful AI solutions.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring an engaging save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to build excitement and highlight AI's role in innovation.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on experiences with agentic AI, offering developers valuable insights and tools. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration in tech development.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which enhances GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour offers interactive sessions for developers to enhance cloud skills through hands-on learning. This initiative aims to foster practical knowledge and immediate application in workflows.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows with features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views, transforming AI into a more tailored coding partner. These enhancements aim to streamline development processes.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, aimed at reducing developer friction. These tools enhance code completion and comprehension, making coding more efficient.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has launched a $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, addressing user demand for a more affordable subscription option. This change aims to attract a broader user base.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a fast Linux distribution that offers a unique approach to preinstalled software, making it stand out among alternatives. Its speed and efficiency may appeal to niche users.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: The Florida Attorney General has launched an investigation into OpenAI following a shooting incident allegedly planned with ChatGPT. This raises concerns about AI's role in real-world violence.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: Broadcom's negative reputation is prompting many VMware customers to migrate to competitors, according to a Western Union executive. This shift highlights the impact of corporate perception on customer loyalty.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Mercor, a startup valued at $10 billion, is facing significant challenges after a data breach, including lawsuits and loss of key customers. This situation underscores the vulnerabilities startups face in cybersecurity.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a feature allowing organization admins to use Copilot directly from security assessment results for contextual guidance. This aims to enhance security management efficiency.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service that enables businesses to automate ongoing tasks using AI agents. This development aims to streamline operations for organizations.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store after the launch of its new model, Muse Spark, indicating strong user interest and engagement. This reflects the competitive landscape of AI applications.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google has decided to continue its partnership with Intel, purchasing SmartNICs for its cloud services instead of developing its own, highlighting a strategic choice amidst rising demand for custom chips.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has restricted the release of its new model, Mythos, citing concerns over its ability to exploit software vulnerabilities. This raises questions about the balance between innovation and security.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are strengthening their AI infrastructure partnership to co-develop custom chips, addressing the growing demand for CPUs amid a global shortage. This collaboration aims to enhance AI capabilities.

item.title: Home Depot will give you a free power tool with this DeWalt deal - what to know
item.description: Home Depot is offering a promotion where customers can receive a free power tool with the purchase of a DeWalt bundle, aiming to boost sales during the lawn care season.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement agencies have disrupted a $45 million cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million in stolen funds and identifying numerous fraud victims. This operation highlights ongoing issues in cryptocurrency security.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared both OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
item.description: A comparison between Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals that the 2026 refresh may not offer significant upgrades to justify skipping last year's model. The analysis highlights performance and features that could influence consumer choices.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub introduces a 'Low Quality' option for moderators to classify hidden comments, enhancing comment management across various platforms. This new feature aims to improve the quality of discussions and streamline moderation.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: The article explains how to manage the lifecycle of models in Amazon Bedrock, detailing transitions and planning strategies to keep AI applications running smoothly. It emphasizes the importance of adapting to evolving models without disruption.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has announced the preview of the AWS Agent Registry, a centralized platform for discovering, sharing, and reusing AI agents and tools within enterprises. This aims to streamline agent management at scale.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: A new 'Sort by' control has been added to GitHub's Notifications page, allowing users to organize notifications from newest to oldest or vice versa. This update enhances user experience by providing more control over notification viewing.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: The article shares tips on transforming an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative, allowing users to access their entire reading library without needing a separate device. This approach emphasizes cost-effectiveness and convenience.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub now displays repository member role labels in the pull request list view, providing clearer visibility of contributors' roles. This feature aims to enhance collaboration and recognition within projects.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: The article provides a guide on embedding a live AI browser agent in a React app using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. It outlines the steps to create an interactive experience for users.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update (26.4.1) introduces Stolen Device Protection by default and fixes a glitch with iCloud syncing, along with standard bug fixes. This update enhances device security and user experience.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The Tubi TV video streaming service now integrates with ChatGPT, allowing users to search for free movies and TV shows using AI. This feature aims to enhance user experience by simplifying content discovery.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 is set to enhance gaming experiences with improved speed and new applications, while also omitting certain features that may appeal to gamers.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub's latest update introduces release tracking in issue sidebars and configurable defaults for project fields, enhancing navigation and usability.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta has modernized WebRTC to avoid issues with forking by creating a dual-stack architecture, ensuring it remains up-to-date with community improvements.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon Bedrock's new MCP client capabilities allow for stateful servers that can engage users and generate dynamic content, enhancing interactivity.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' has been released on GeForce NOW, allowing players to stream it on various devices with enhanced cinematic quality.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: NVIDIA celebrates National Robotics Week by showcasing advancements in physical AI, highlighting robots' transformative impact across various industries.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub's March 2026 availability report details four incidents that affected service performance, indicating ongoing reliability challenges.

item.title: CyberAgent moves faster with ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex
item.description: CyberAgent is leveraging ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to enhance AI adoption and decision-making speed in its advertising and gaming sectors.

item.title: OpenAI Full Fan Mode Contest: Terms & Conditions
item.description: The OpenAI Full Fan Mode Contest outlines entry requirements and prizes, encouraging participation through social media.

item.title: A Theoretical Framework for Acoustic Neighbor Embeddings
item.description: This paper presents a theoretical framework for understanding acoustic neighbor embeddings, which can enhance phonetic analysis in audio processing.

item.title: LaCy: What Small Language Models Can and Should Learn is Not Just a Question of Loss
item.description: A paper presented at ICLR discusses the limitations of Small Language Models (SLMs) in knowledge compression and suggests that external querying can help mitigate factual inaccuracies. This highlights the ongoing challenges in improving the performance of smaller models.

item.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
item.description: Waypoint-1.5 introduces higher-fidelity interactive worlds designed for everyday GPUs, enhancing the experience for developers and users alike. This advancement could significantly improve the quality of interactive applications.

item.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
item.description: The article discusses the development of multimodal embedding and reranker models using Sentence Transformers, which can improve the performance of various AI tasks. This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating different data types for better model outcomes.

item.title: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description: GitHub's Copilot usage metrics API now includes new metrics for pull request merges reviewed by Copilot, building on previous metrics for pull request throughput. This enhancement provides deeper insights into the effectiveness of AI-assisted coding.

item.title: Anthropic Supply-Chain Risk Label Should Stay In Place, Appeals Court Says
item.description: An appeals court ruled that Anthropic's supply-chain risk label should remain in place, complicating the company's use of its AI model, Claude, by the US military. This decision reflects ongoing legal challenges in AI deployment for defense.

item.title: US court declines to block Pentagon's Anthropic blacklisting for now
item.description: A federal appeals court has declined to block the Pentagon's blacklisting of Anthropic, marking a temporary victory for the Trump administration in its national security concerns. This ruling adds to the legal complexities surrounding AI use in defense.

item.title: Show HN: I built an open source multi-agent harness in Go
item.description: A developer has open-sourced a multi-agent harness built in Go, designed to orchestrate multiple AI agents collaboratively. This tool aims to enhance the efficiency of AI workflows and team interactions.

item.title: broccoli-ml 21.0.0
item.description: The release of broccoli-ml 21.0.0 introduces new PyTorch models, showcasing advancements in machine learning tools. This update is aimed at improving accessibility and functionality for developers.

item.title: Anthropic loses appeals court bid to block Pentagon blacklisting temporarily
item.description: The D.C. Circuit has denied Anthropic's request to temporarily block the Pentagon's blacklisting, continuing the legal battle over the use of its AI model, Claude. This ruling underscores the ongoing scrutiny of AI in military applications.

item.title: D.C. Circuit Declines to Stay Department of War's "Supply-Chain Risk" Designation of Claude
item.description: The D.C. Circuit confirmed the Department of War's designation of a supply-chain risk for Anthropic's Claude, denying a stay in the ongoing legal case. This decision highlights the regulatory challenges faced by AI firms.
